## [00:00 - 03:02]

Operation Quick Start, this is the mental game part two, which is all about how I keep my mental game intact. So I talked about mental game or your inner game and how that's the absolute most important thing for your success as an entrepreneur. It's not what's happening outside your body, really, outside of your mind. It's not the external factors. It's not what your competitors are doing, what the market is doing, what the economy is doing. It's what's happening inside your head and staying at a high mental state so that you can continue to create, that you can continue to overcome, that you can keep moving forward, that you can keep your momentum, that you can regain your momentum if you lose it, that you can deal with setbacks. That's what mental game is and it is the most important thing, the most important factor in your success. In your getting started and in your maintaining, getting momentum, maintaining momentum and then just having the stamina to keep your business going. It's been over 20 years for me and the one thing I can, I feel good about telling you is that I've got really good stamina and I can keep on grinding away and the reason I keep grinding away, I can keep on grinding away, is because I work really hard on my mental game. I work really hard on my inner game and I've built in a lot of things within my life to keep my mental game, if not at a peak, at least at a very high level and that's what this section is about. So one of the most important things and I'm coming up on 10 years where I've really focused on this is my morning ritual. So every morning when I wake up, I have this a ritual, I have these habits that I go through that set me up for a really strong powerful day. So the first thing I do or what really the first thing I do is is oral hygiene, brushing my teeth and flossing my teeth and hydrating, drinking 16 ounces of water. Then, so I mean, it's simple, but you know, right now there's so much research coming out about the importance of oral hygiene and for your entire body and part of your mental game is staying healthy. If you're not healthy, then if you're getting colds, if you're getting sick, if you're getting the flu, it's you just can't create, you can't stay on top of your game if you're getting sick. So I'm a big fan of oral hygiene, staying hydrated and then right after that I go right into yoga. I do about 15 to 20 minutes, very light yoga,

## [03:02 - 06:04]

nothing too intense. I do it on my own, I have my own home practice. I basically watch some videos and developed a practice. Then the next part of my morning ritual is meditation and I usually do 20 minutes of meditation in the morning. ## [06:04 - 09:09]

that's something I reflect back on. But in the morning it's just sort of about clearing your mind. Another habit that I have is what I call the three wins. And I learned this from my coach Dan Sullivan and I do this, a lot of times I'll do it when I go to bed. Most of the time when I go to bed, sometimes when I wake up. And what I do is I write down three wins I had that day. And then three wins I'm going to have the next day. Or if I'm doing it in the morning, it's three wins I had yesterday and three wins I'm going to have today. And the wins don't have to be epic. They could be really simple. But the idea is that you are setting your day up as a win. But that one in the win column. So it could be, I just had a wonderful hug in the morning with my wife. Or it could be I had a great meditation session. Or it could be I had a great dinner. Or it could be I created my lead magnet. I mean it could be business, it could be big, it could be small. But no matter what, you can find three things that went well in it in any day. There are always three wins to be found. And so this is just a practice of finding and recognizing your wins. And then when you're setting up wins for the next day, when you're writing those down, they don't have to be epic huge like amazing wins. Keep them simple. Make it so that you can achieve them. And by the way, if you don't achieve them the next night or the next morning, when you're writing down your three wins, you don't even have to look back and see what you anticipated your wins were going to be. It doesn't matter. Write down three wins that you had. And if you're on right four or five, that's fine as well. So in other words, I go to bed tonight, I write down three wins I had or three wins I'm going to have tomorrow. Then tomorrow, even if I don't have those three wins, if I have three other wins, it doesn't matter. I just write them down. And this is, it's, this is a really powerful practice. And if you start doing this every day, you'll set yourself up to just find the positive. And that can be super, super powerful. This doesn't mean you're sticking your head in the sand and it's you're all about polyana and everything's perfect. It's just finding the wins that you had. And then a lot of times when I'm writing those down, I'll write down two or three things that I'm grateful for. Two or three things that, that I've gratitude for. And of gratitude practices, immensely powerful. Now, another thing I do is regular body work. Meaning I get regular massages, ralphing. I do float sessions in sensory deprivation tanks.
